title: Cyclic ladder operators and hidden Weyl-Heisenberg structure in a Floquet system
abstract: Ladder operators, found in the quantum harmonic oscillator and other quantized systems, provide an elegant approach to solving or understanding otherwise intricate physics problems. In this Letter, we discuss cyclic ladder operators in both Hermitian and non-Hermitian systems with a finite Hilbert space, with the highest (lowest) level directly descending (ascending) to the lowest (highest) level via a single raising (lowering) operation. We show that an equally spaced energy ladder emerges when these systems have an underlying Weyl-Heisenberg commutation relation, with the cyclic ladder operators and the temporal evolution operator behaving as the generators of the Weyl-Heisenberg group. We further illustrate such a system using a one-dimensional Floquet lattice, where the cyclic ladder operators become diagonal and the temporal evolution simplifies to a permutation matrix after a Floquet period. Our findings reveal a hidden relation between non-trivial dynamics and algebraic principles in Floquet systems, which may exist for other quantum numbers as well besides the energy levels.
summary: This theoretical paper explores cyclic ladder operators in finite quantum systems, showing that if these systems obey a Weyl-Heisenberg commutation relation, their energy spectrum must be equally spaced. This principle is rigorously demonstrated using a one-dimensional Floquet lattice model, revealing a deep algebraic connection between the system's time evolution and its underlying discrete symmetries.
main_problem: The paper investigates cyclic ladder operators in finite-dimensional quantum systems, particularly exploring how an underlying Weyl-Heisenberg (WH) commutation relation dictates the energy spectrum and dynamics.
main_result: It demonstrates that the existence of a WH structure forces the system to possess an equally spaced energy ladder, which is confirmed by showing the temporal evolution operator relates directly to the cyclic ladder operators in specific limits.
method: The analysis involves deriving algebraic properties of generalized ladder operators for finite Hilbert spaces and applying these concepts to model dynamics using Floquet theory.
model_or_system: The primary models are general quantum systems with discrete spectra, exemplified by a one-dimensional tight-binding lattice subjected to periodic driving (Floquet system).
key_observables: Equally spaced energy ladder ($E_n$), the temporal evolution operator $U(T)$, and the diagonal form of cyclic ladder operators.
important_parameters: The number of sites ($N$) in the lattice, the time period ($T$), and the coupling strengths ($	heta$).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the existence or emergence of an underlying Weyl-Heisenberg commutation relation to enforce spectral regularity.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate schematics of the 1D Floquet system, its band structures under varying driving conditions (including Dirac cone formation), and particle movement trajectories on a finite lattice.
paper_structure: The paper first introduces cyclic ladder operators in general finite systems, establishing the link to WH algebra. It then applies this framework to a specific one-dimensional Floquet lattice model, analyzing how the time evolution operator $U(T)$ manifests these algebraic symmetries.
why_interesting: This work provides deep connections between discrete spectral properties (equally spaced ladders) and fundamental algebraic structures (WH group), offering insights into the underlying symmetries governing dynamics in periodically driven quantum systems relevant to AMO physics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Schematics of a one-dimensional Floquet system with temporal period T and spatial period Λ. Couplings are marked by bars, and one unit cell is boxed by the dashed line. Sublattices A and B are marked by filled and open dots. (b-d) Band structure of the Floquet system with g = 1 and T/π = 0.9, 1, 1.1, respectively. The first Floquet BZ is darkened.
